rag_rules: Assignment statements in AGREE must be placed within component implementation scopes, not type scopes.
AGREE property associations and references must point to valid identifiers present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts should be placed in appropriate annex blocks matching the component scope: type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the original model.
When resolving references in AGREE, ensure all identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex block.
Use only valid AADL feature names or declared auxiliary variables in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignment statements in AGREE are valid only in implementation scopes.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not place assignment statements in component type annexes; they are only valid in implementation scopes.
Do not reference identifiers in AGREE that are not declared or visible in the same annex block.
